search_terms_used: high intensity interval training VO2max running systematic review meta-analysis
interval training work rest ratio distance runners performance adaptations
Billat 2001 interval training VO2max time running review
Buchheit Laursen 2013 high intensity interval training solutions
sprint interval training distance running performance SIT endurance
coverage_notes: Strong coverage from foundational reviews (Laursen 2002, Billat 2001) through modern meta-analyses (Milanović 2015, Bacon 2013). Includes HIIT programming framework (Buchheit & Laursen 2013 two-parter), running-specific studies, and sprint interval training. Good mix of VO2max-focused and practical prescription papers.

papers.title: Interval training at VO2max: effects on aerobic performance and overtraining markers
papers.notes: Examined 4-week interval training at vVO2max in runners, measuring aerobic performance changes and overtraining markers.

papers.title: Interval training for performance: a scientific and empirical practice. Special recommendations for middle- and long-distance running. Part I: aerobic interval training
papers.notes: Comprehensive review of aerobic interval training science with specific recommendations for middle- and long-distance runners.

papers.title: The scientific basis for high-intensity interval training: optimising training programmes and maximising performance in highly trained endurance athletes
papers.notes: Reviewed the scientific basis for HIIT in highly trained athletes where additional submaximal volume no longer improves performance.

papers.title: Is there an optimal training intensity for enhancing the maximal oxygen uptake of distance runners?: empirical research findings, current opinions, physiological rationale and practical recommendations
papers.notes: Reviewed empirical evidence and physiological rationale for optimal training intensity to enhance VO2max in distance runners.

papers.title: Aerobic high-intensity intervals improve VO2max more than moderate training
papers.notes: RCT comparing four training protocols (LSD, LT, 15/15, 4x4min) in 40 moderately trained men showing 4x4min intervals most effective for VO2max.

papers.title: Adaptations to aerobic interval training: interactive effects of exercise intensity and total work duration
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: RCT of 35 cyclists comparing 4x4min, 4x8min, and 4x16min interval durations to determine interaction of intensity and work duration.

papers.title: High-intensity interval training, solutions to the programming puzzle: Part I: cardiopulmonary emphasis
papers.notes: Part 1 of comprehensive HIIT programming framework addressing cardiopulmonary adaptations and work/rest manipulation variables.

papers.title: High-intensity interval training, solutions to the programming puzzle. Part II: anaerobic energy, neuromuscular load and practical applications
papers.notes: Part 2 of HIIT programming framework covering anaerobic energy systems, neuromuscular load, and practical training applications.

papers.title: VO2max trainability and high intensity interval training in humans: a meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 37 studies (334 subjects) examining VO2max improvements from interval training programs published 1965-2012.

papers.title: Effectiveness of High-Intensity Interval Training (HIT) and Continuous Endurance Training for VO2max Improvements: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Controlled Trials
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 28 controlled trials comparing HIIT vs continuous endurance training for VO2max improvements.

papers.title: Six Sessions of Sprint Interval Training Improves Running Performance in Trained Athletes
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Examined whether just six sprint interval training sessions could improve 3km time trial and other performance measures in trained trail runners.

papers.title: Physiological basis of interval training for performance enhancement
papers.notes: Review of the physiological mechanisms underlying interval training adaptations for performance enhancement.
